epigraph.text: Do not be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewal of your mind.
epigraph.quote: A change of variables often transforms a difficult problem into a simple one.

content.text:
When we apply functions to random variables, how do their 
distributions change?
Functions of a Single Random Variable
If $Y = g(X)$, how do we find the distribution of $Y$?

**Discrete case:** List the possible values of $Y$ and sum 
the probabilities.

content.title: Transformation of Discrete RV
content.problem:
If $X \in \{-1, 0, 1\}$ with equal probability $\frac{1}{3}$ each, 
and $Y = X^2$, find the PMF of $Y$.

content.title: Transformation Formula
content.content:
If $Y = g(X)$ where $g$ is strictly monotonic and differentiable:
\[
 f_Y(y) = f_X(g^{-1}(y)) \cdot \left| \frac{d}{dy} g^{-1}(y) \right|
\]
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Logarithmic Transformation
content.solution:
$g(x) = \ln(x)$, so $g^{-1}(y) = e^y$ and $\frac{d}{dy} e^y = e^y$.

$f_X(x) = e^{-x}$ for $x > 0$.

\[
 f_Y(y) = e^{-e^y} \cdot e^y = e^{y - e^y} \quad \text{for } -\infty < y < \infty
\]

content.text:
**Discrete:**
\[
 P(Z = z) = \sum_x P(X = x) \cdot P(Y = z - x)
\]

content.title: Sum of Uniform Random Variables
content.problem:
If $X, Y \sim \Unif(0, 1)$ independently, the PDF of $Z = X + Y$ 
is triangular:
\[
 f_Z(z) = \begin{cases}
 z & 0 < z < 1 \\
 2 - z & 1 \leq z < 2
 \end{cases}
\]
